children.heading: Repayment rates for upland cotton, long grain rice, and medium grain rice
children.label: b Repayment rates for upland cotton, long grain rice, and medium grain rice
A
children.text: the loan rate established for the commodity under , plus interest (determined in accordance with ); orsection 9032 of this titlesection 7283 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of long grain rice and medium grain rice, the prevailing world market price for the commodity, as determined and adjusted by the Secretary in accordance with this section; or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of upland cotton, the prevailing world market price for the commodity, as determined and adjusted by the Secretary in accordance with this section.

children.heading: Refund for upland cotton
children.label: 2 Refund for upland cotton
children.text: In the case of a repayment for a marketing assistance loan for upland cotton at a rate described in paragraph (1)(B)(ii), the Secretary shall provide to the producer a refund (if any) in an amount equal to the difference between the lowest prevailing world market price, as determined and adjusted by the Secretary in accordance with this section, during the 30-day period following the date on which the producer repays the marketing assistance loan and the repayment rate.

children.heading: Repayment rates for extra long staple cotton
children.label: c Repayment rates for extra long staple cotton

children.text: the loan rate established for the commodity under , plus interest (determined in accordance with ); andsection 9032 of this titlesection 7283 of this title
the prevailing world market price for the commodity, as determined and adjusted by the Secretary in accordance with this section.

children.label: A
children.text: a formula to determine the prevailing world market price for each of upland cotton, long grain rice, medium grain rice, and extra long staple cotton; and

children.label: B
children.text: a mechanism by which the Secretary shall announce periodically those prevailing world market prices.

children.heading: Upland cotton
children.label: 2 Upland cotton
children.text: In the case of upland cotton, for any period when price quotations for Middling (M) 1⁄-inch cotton are available, the formula under paragraph (1)(A) shall be based on the average of the 3 lowest-priced growths that are quoted.332

children.heading: Adjustment of prevailing world market price for upland cotton, extra long staple cotton, long grain rice, and medium grain rice
children.label: e Adjustment of prevailing world market price for upland cotton, extra long staple cotton, long grain rice, and medium grain rice

children.heading: Rice
children.label: 1 Rice
children.text: The prevailing world market price for long grain rice and medium grain rice determined under subsection (d) shall be adjusted to United States quality and location.

children.label: i
children.text: a reduction equal to any United States Premium Factor for upland cotton of a quality higher than Middling (M) 1⁄-inch; and332

children.label: ii
children.text: the average costs to market the commodity, including average transportation costs, as determined by the Secretary; and

children.label: i
children.text: to minimize potential loan forfeitures;

children.label: ii
children.text: to minimize the accumulation of stocks of upland cotton by the Federal Government;

children.label: iii
children.text: to ensure that upland cotton produced in the United States can be marketed freely and competitively, both domestically and internationally; and

children.label: I
children.text: there are insufficient current-crop price quotations; and

children.label: II
children.text: the forward-crop price quotation is the lowest such quotation available.

children.label: A
children.text: shall be adjusted to United States quality and location, with the adjustment to include the average costs to market the commodity, including average transportation costs, as determined by the Secretary; and

children.label: i
children.text: to minimize potential loan forfeitures;

children.label: ii
children.text: to minimize the accumulation of stocks of extra long staple cotton by the Federal Government;

children.label: iii
children.text: to ensure that extra long staple cotton produced in the United States can be marketed freely and competitively; and

children.label: I
children.text: there are insufficient current-crop price quotations; and

children.label: II
children.text: the forward-crop price quotation is the lowest such quotation available.

children.heading: Guidelines for additional adjustments
children.label: 4 Guidelines for additional adjustments
children.text: In making adjustments under this subsection, the Secretary shall establish a mechanism for determining and announcing the adjustments in order to avoid undue disruption in the United States market.

children.heading: Repayment rates for confectionery and other kinds of sunflower seeds
children.label: f Repayment rates for confectionery and other kinds of sunflower seeds

children.text: the loan rate established for the commodity under , plus interest (determined in accordance with ); orsection 9032 of this titlesection 7283 of this title
the repayment rate established for oil sunflower seed.

children.heading: Payment of cotton storage costs
children.label: g Payment of cotton storage costs

children.heading: Crop years 2014 through 2025
children.label: 1 Crop years 2014 through 2025
children.text: Effective for each of the 2014 through 2025 crop years, the Secretary shall make cotton storage payments available in the same manner, and at the same rates as the Secretary provided storage payments for the 2006 crop of cotton, except that the rates shall be reduced by 10 percent.

children.label: A
children.text: the submitted storage charge for the current marketing year; and

children.label: i
children.text: California or Arizona, a payment rate of $4.90; and

children.label: ii
children.text: any other State, a payment rate of $3.00.
the loan rate established for peanuts under subsection (a)(20) or (b)(20), as applicable, of , plus interest (determined in accordance with ); orsection 9032 of this titlesection 7283 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: minimize potential loan forfeitures;

children.label: B
children.text: minimize the accumulation of stocks of peanuts by the Federal Government;

children.label: C
children.text: minimize the cost incurred by the Federal Government in storing peanuts; and

children.label: D
children.text: allow peanuts produced in the United States to be marketed freely and competitively, both domestically and internationally.

children.heading: Authority to temporarily adjust repayment rates
children.label: i Authority to temporarily adjust repayment rates

children.heading: Adjustment authority
children.label: 1 Adjustment authority
children.text: In the event of a severe disruption to marketing, transportation, or related infrastructure, the Secretary may modify the repayment rate otherwise applicable under this section for marketing assistance loans under for a loan commodity.section 9031 of this title

children.heading: Duration
children.label: 2 Duration
children.text: Any adjustment made under paragraph (1) in the repayment rate for marketing assistance loans for a loan commodity shall be in effect on a short-term and temporary basis, as determined by the Secretary.
